title: tragacantha elongata
intro: Tragacantha elongata, also known as the 'broom snakeweed', is a perennial shrub native to parts of Africa and Europe, characterized by its long, slender stems and small yellow flowers. It possesses medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to inhibit certain inflammatory enzymes, which may contribute to its therapeutic potential. The health benefits associated with Tragacantha elongata include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of certain conditions, such as arthritis and asthma. Tragacantha elongata is often prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal purposes, although further research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential side effects.
